title: Hakea rubra
taxonomy: Hakea rubra is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It belongs to the genus Hakea and is native to Western Australia. The genus Hakea is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as needle bush or hakea. The family Proteaceae is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Southern Hemisphere. Hakea rubra is classified as a shrub or small tree that produces red flowers.

intro: Hakea rubra, also known as Red Needlewood, is a shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Proteaceae. The plant has been traditionally used by the indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant benefits, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hakea rubra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
